duration_trend.note: Very tight duration band (25-39 min) across all 8, unlike the wide-swing documentary channels in this niche-intel set (LEMMiNO, Fern). No real trend direction -- oldest-4 and newest-4 averages differ by only ~3 minutes, well within normal episode-to-episode variance. This reflects a factory-cadence, standardized-runtime format rather than a 'flagship documentary' model: MrBallen uploads studio narration episodes plus a distinct shorter-form 'Campfire Stories with MrBallen' sub-series (27:05, and per Community posts, a regular Friday/weekend slot) that keeps the average anchored near 30 min.

palette_trend: Consistent high-saturation, high-contrast thumbnail identity: dark/black backgrounds with a single dramatic color-graded photo or illustrated scene (fire orange, sickly green, blood red, UFO-beam teal) plus bold white/yellow sans-serif caption text overlaid directly on the image. Faces/human figures are frequently featured (unlike LEMMiNO's faceless vector style), often in shadow or partial silhouette for a true-crime/thriller mood. 'Campfire Stories' sub-series thumbnails use a warm firelight orange as a recognizable sub-brand cue. Very on-trend with mainstream true-crime/mystery YouTube thumbnail conventions (drop-shadow text, arrow/circle annotations not needed because the photo itself does the work) -- optimized hard for CTR rather than for a distinct minimalist identity.
